fields.icd_description: Tear of unspecified meniscus, right knee, initial encounter
fields.icd_conflict_detail: The primary diagnosis code differs across all three documents: the referral form uses M23.611 (Spontaneous disruption of anterior cruciate ligament, right knee), while both the clinical notes and prescription use S83.209A (Tear of unspecified meniscus, right knee, initial encounter). Additionally, the clinical notes reference a surgical procedure of ACL Reconstruction, creating a clinical inconsistency between the procedure performed and the meniscus tear diagnosis code. The secondary pain code also conflicts: the referral form lists G89.11 (Acute pain due to trauma) while the clinical notes and prescription use G89.18 (Other acute postprocedural pain). The knee stiffness laterality also conflicts: clinical notes cite M25.362 (left knee stiffness) while the referral form and prescription cite M25.361 (right knee stiffness). S83.209A was selected as the primary code per clinical notes and prescription authority, but the ACL vs. meniscus discrepancy warrants escalation.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription agree on S83.209A, overriding the referral form's M23.611, but the clinical notes simultaneously document ACL Reconstruction surgery — not a meniscus procedure — creating an unresolvable clinical inconsistency that requires physician clarification before dispatch.

icd_conflicts.label: Pain Classification
icd_conflicts.reasoning: G89.18 (Other acute postprocedural pain) is appropriate given the documented post-surgical context and is supported by both clinical notes and prescription, superseding the referral form's G89.11 (Acute pain due to trauma).

icd_conflicts.label: Knee Stiffness Laterality
icd_conflicts.reasoning: The referral form and prescription both cite M25.361 (right knee stiffness), which is clinically consistent with the documented right knee injury and surgery, but the clinical notes cite M25.362 (left knee stiffness), a likely documentation error that nonetheless creates a laterality conflict requiring physician confirmation.

fields.priority_reason: Post-surgical ACL reconstruction case with unsafe ambulation documented at pain level 9/10 on weight-bearing, multiple ICD code conflicts with confidence below 80 requiring escalation, and an unresolved equipment weight-capacity safety concern for a 285 lb patient on a standard rollator.
fields.notes: 1. WEIGHT CAPACITY ALERT: Patient weighs 285 lbs, which approaches or exceeds the standard rollator (E0143) weight limit (typically 250–300 lbs depending on manufacturer). Clinical notes explicitly flag this and recommend confirming a bariatric rollator (400 lb capacity) with the case manager before dispatch. A bariatric rollator may require a different HCPCS code and separate authorization. 2. AUTHORIZATION MISSING: No authorization reference number has been provided on the referral form. Authorization from Pacific Mutual Workers Comp must be obtained prior to delivery. 3. ICD CODE CONFLICT — ESCALATE: Primary diagnosis code conflicts across documents (M23.611 on referral form vs. S83.209A on clinical notes and prescription), and the documented surgical procedure (ACL Reconstruction) is inconsistent with the meniscus tear diagnosis code (S83.209A) in the notes and prescription. Physician clarification is required. 4. KNEE STIFFNESS LATERALITY — ESCALATE: Clinical notes document left knee stiffness (M25.362) while referral form and prescription document right knee stiffness (M25.361); likely a documentation error but must be confirmed. 5. Preferred appointment window and transportation fields are blank — follow up with adjuster Linda Torres at (714) 555-0182 or l.torres@pacificmutual.com.

fired_rules.source: ICD-10-CM Guidelines §I.C.19.a — Initial vs Subsequent Encounter
fired_rules.message: S83.209A is the correct post-surgical ACL encounter code per ICD-10-CM §I.C.19.a.
G89.11 rejected — G89.11 (acute pain due to trauma) is incorrect post-procedure. Pain following surgery is classified G89.18 (other acute postprocedural pain) per §I.C.6.b.

fired_rules.source: ICD-10-CM Guidelines §I.C.6.b.1 — Postprocedural Pain Not Associated with Specific Postoperative Complication
fired_rules.message: G89.18 confirmed as correct acute postprocedural pain code per §I.C.6.b.1.

Laterality conflict detected: M23.611 vs S83.209A, M25.361 vs M25.362 — Laterality mismatch across documents. Per §I.B.13, the treating physician clinical notes are authoritative — but conflicting documents require clinical review before dispatch.

fired_rules.source: CMS LCD L33803 — Walkers, Standard and Heavy Duty
fired_rules.message: S83.209A qualifies as a covered diagnosis for E0143 per CMS LCD L33803.

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

fired_rules.source: CMS HCPCS Level II — E0143 vs E0149 Differentiation
fired_rules.message: Patient weight 285 lbs — Patient weight 285 lbs — within 15 lbs of E0143 standard capacity (300 lbs). Confirm bariatric specification with prescribing physician before dispatch to avoid wrong-equipment delivery.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Linda Torres,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a pending durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured, **James Holloway**, under workers' compensation claim **WC-2026-084431**.

In reviewing the referral, we have identified the following missing information required to process this request:

- **Authorization Reference Number** *(auth_ref)* — **Hard block; routing is on hold pending receipt**
- **Appointment Window** *(appt_window)* — **Hard block; routing is on hold pending receipt**
- **Transportation Arrangements** *(transportation)* — Required to complete the referral record

Please note that the two items marked as hard blocks are preventing us from moving this referral forward. We are unable to schedule or route services until both are received.

To avoid delays in care for Mr. Holloway, we kindly request that all outstanding information be provided **within 24 business hours** of this communication. If you have any questions or require clarification on any of the items listed above, please do not hesitate to contact our office directly.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. We appreciate your partnership and look forward to your response.

Warm regards,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME
Intake & Referral Services
